name: bluewalker
summary:
Bluewalker is a proof-of-concept Bluetooth LE scanner and advertiser which uses
_HCI user channel_ raw HCI sockets available on Linux to scan for Bluetooth LE
devices or to start advertising with user-specified data. User channel sockets
allow raw access to the Bluetooth controller and bypasses the Linux Bluetooth
stack. This allows Bluewalker access to all Bluetooth LE Advertisement Data sent
by nearby Bluetooth LE devices.

documentation.readme:
Bluewalker is a proof-of-concept Bluetooth LE scanner and advertiser which uses
_HCI user channel_ raw HCI sockets available on Linux to scan for Bluetooth LE
devices or to start advertising with user-specified data. User channel sockets
allow raw access to the Bluetooth controller and bypasses the Linux Bluetooth
stack. This allows Bluewalker access to all Bluetooth LE Advertisement Data sent
by nearby Bluetooth LE devices.

arguments.name: --index
arguments.description: CLI flag '--index' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ukBaz/python-bluezero (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tinygo-org/bluetooth (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with souravbaghz/ble-name-exploitor (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'any' phase against a target with '['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh']' as the attack surface (bluetooth, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the Bluetooth category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Bluetooth category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Bluetooth system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
